How old were you when you started using hormone replacement therapy? 6. Are you still taking hormone replacement therapy? 6. How many times have you been pregnant, including live births, stillbirths, spontaneous miscarriage or therapeutic abortions? 7. How many children have you given birth to, considering live births only? 8. How old were you when you last became pregnant? 9. How old were you when you first became pregnant? 10. How old were you when you started using hormonal contraceptives?

"Ever had a Miscarriage " "Ever had a Abortion or termination (for personal reasons) " "Ever had a Abortion or termination (for medical reasons) " "Ever had a Ectopic pregnancy (tubal pregnancy) " "Ever had a Live birth " "Ever had a Still birth

Wording of Related Questions\/Variables: Entry criteria included age, at least one period in past 12 months, uterus intact and at least 1 ovary. Women were studied up to 5 years postmenopause

The inclusion criteria for this longitudinal study were women from the baseline study for whom menopausal status was able to be determined (had menses in the previous 3 months, had an intact uterus with at least one ovary, and were not taking oral contraceptives or hormone therapy).

FROM CODEBOOK: Uterus: is a numeric value that is 0 if the subject had no uterus when she enteredthe BIMORA study (usually in January 1998), and is 1 if she had a uterus.A value of –1 means this status is unknown. Note that if the subject has her uterus removed after enrollment, it will be reported as an event. Ovary: is a numeric value that is 0 if the subject had no ovaries when she entered the BIMORA study (usually 1998), is 1 if she had only one ovary, and 2 if she had both ovaries. Note that if the subject has one or both ovaries removed after enrollment, it will be reported as an event code. Surgery: Hysterectomy Surgery: Panhysterectomy (uterus and both ovaries removed) Surgery: Unilateral oophorectomy Surgery: Bilateral oophorectomy (or second ovary removed)
Other Women's Health Questions: Menopausal symptoms, HRT use, infertility

Wording of Related Questions\/Variables: Have you ever had any of the following gynecologic surgeries?: Cervical surgery (removal of part or all of the cervix), Hysterectomy (removal of uterus), Oopherectomy, unilateral (removal of only one ovary), Oopherectomy, bilateral (removal of both ovaries)

Female Sample Adults 40-70 who have not had a hysterectomy, have not had ovaries removed, are notcurrently pregnant and have not had a live birth in the past 12 months, or answered DK or REF to any of these questions The next questions are about menopause. In the past 12 months, have you experienced any of these symptoms associated with menopause?
